title: Create and Retain System Audit Logs
description: Create and retain system audit logs and records to the extent needed to enable the monitoring, analysis, investigation, and reporting of unlawful or unauthorized system activity.

checks.name: Audit Policy is Enabled
checks.purpose: Verify that advanced audit policy subcategories are configured and enabled on the Windows client

checks.name: Security Event Log Exists and is Active
checks.purpose: Confirm the Security event log is enabled, writable, and actively receiving events

checks.name: Audit Log Retention Policy is Configured
checks.purpose: Verify the Security event log is configured with a minimum retention size and retention method (overwrite by days or do not overwrite)

checks.name: Audit Logs are Being Written
checks.purpose: Confirm recent events are present in the Security log to verify active logging is occurring

checks.name: Audit Policy is Enabled
checks.purpose: Verify that advanced audit policy subcategories are configured and enabled on the Windows server

checks.name: Security Event Log Exists and is Active
checks.purpose: Confirm the Security event log is enabled, writable, and actively receiving events

checks.name: Audit Log Retention Policy is Configured
checks.purpose: Verify the Security event log is configured with a minimum retention size and days-based retention policy

checks.name: Audit Logs are Being Written
checks.purpose: Confirm recent events are present in the Security log to verify active logging is occurring

checks.name: Logs are Forwarded to Centralized Log Store
checks.purpose: Verify Windows Event Forwarding (WEF) or a SIEM agent is configured to ship logs off the host for centralized retention

checks.name: Auditd Service is Running
checks.purpose: Verify the auditd daemon is active and enabled at boot

checks.name: Audit Rules are Loaded
checks.expected_result: Non-empty list of audit rules covering file access, user actions, and privilege escalation
checks.purpose: Confirm audit rules are loaded and actively monitoring required event categories

checks.name: Audit Log File Exists and is Being Written
checks.purpose: Verify /var/log/audit/audit.log exists, is non-empty, and has recent entries

checks.name: Audit Log Retention is Configured
checks.purpose: Check /etc/audit/auditd.conf for max_log_file, num_logs, and max_log_file_action to confirm retention meets policy

checks.name: Logs are Forwarded to Centralized Log Store
checks.purpose: Verify rsyslog, syslog-ng, or auditd audisp-remote plugin is configured to ship logs to a centralized SIEM or log server

checks.name: Auditd Service is Running
checks.purpose: Verify the auditd daemon is active and enabled at boot

checks.name: Audit Rules are Loaded
checks.expected_result: Non-empty list of audit rules covering file access, user actions, and privilege escalation
checks.purpose: Confirm audit rules are loaded and actively monitoring required event categories

checks.name: Audit Log File Exists and is Being Written
checks.purpose: Verify /var/log/audit/audit.log exists, is non-empty, and has recent entries

checks.name: Audit Log Retention is Configured
checks.purpose: Check /etc/audit/auditd.conf for max_log_file, num_logs, and max_log_file_action to confirm retention meets policy

checks.name: Logs are Forwarded to Centralized Log Store
checks.purpose: Verify rsyslog or auditd audisp-remote plugin is configured to ship logs to a centralized SIEM or log server

remediation: Enable advanced audit policies via Group Policy or auditpol. Configure auditd on Linux with appropriate rules. Set log retention size and duration. Configure log forwarding to a centralized SIEM.
